audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Sinai arrival, eagle-wing deliverance, treasured possession, priestly kingdom, holy nation, boundary/holiness, thunder-cloud theophany.

verses.restored: In third month after children of Israel went out from land of Egypt, on this day, they came to wilderness of Sinai.
They journeyed from Rephidim and came to wilderness of Sinai and camped in wilderness; Israel camped there before the mountain.
Moses went up to the Divine, and YHWH called to him from mountain, saying, Thus you shall say to house of Jacob and tell children of Israel:
You yourselves saw what I did to Egypt, and I carried you on wings of eagles and brought you to myself.
And now, if listening you listen to my voice and keep my covenant, then you shall be my treasured possession from all peoples, for all earth is mine.
And you shall be to me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation. These are the words you shall speak to children of Israel.
Moses came and called elders of the people and placed before them all these words that YHWH commanded him.
All the people answered together and said, All that YHWH has spoken we will do. Moses returned words of the people to YHWH.
YHWH said to Moses, Look, I am coming to you in thickness of cloud, so the people may hear when I speak with you and also trust in you into the age. Moses told words of people to YHWH.
YHWH said to Moses, Go to the people and sanctify them today and tomorrow; let them wash their garments.
Let them be ready for third day, for on third day YHWH will come down before eyes of all people upon Mount Sinai.
You shall set boundary for the people all around, saying, Guard yourselves from going up mountain or touching its edge. Everyone touching mountain shall surely be put to death.
No hand shall touch him, but stoning he shall be stoned or shooting he shall be shot; whether beast or human, one shall not live. When ram's horn draws long, they may go up to mountain.
Moses went down from mountain to people and sanctified the people, and they washed their garments.
He said to the people, Be ready for third day; do not approach a woman.
It happened on third day, when morning came, there were voices/thunders and flashes, heavy cloud upon mountain, and very strong voice of horn; all people in camp trembled.
Moses brought the people out from camp to meet the Divine, and they stationed themselves at foot of mountain.
Mount Sinai was all smoke because YHWH descended upon it in fire; its smoke went up like smoke of kiln, and whole mountain trembled greatly.
Voice of horn grew stronger and stronger; Moses spoke, and the Divine answered him in voice.
YHWH came down upon Mount Sinai, to top of mountain; YHWH called Moses to top of mountain, and Moses went up.
YHWH said to Moses, Go down, warn the people lest they break through to YHWH to see, and many of them fall.
Also priests who come near to YHWH must sanctify themselves, lest YHWH break out upon them.
Moses said to YHWH, The people cannot come up to Mount Sinai, because you yourself warned us, saying, Set boundary around mountain and sanctify it.
YHWH said to him, Go, go down, and you shall come up, you and Aaron with you; but priests and people shall not break through to come up to YHWH, lest he break out upon them.
Moses went down to the people and said to them.

translation_notes: Covenant identity is grounded in prior liberation: eagle wings before obedience.
Treasured possession does not cancel the wider claim 'all earth is mine.' Election is vocation, not supremacy.
Kingdom of priests / holy nation is communal vocation toward Divine service.
Boundary language marks holiness and danger of theophany; avoid weaponizing it as exclusion beyond the narrative.
safety_note: Sinai holiness should not be used for coercive spiritual hierarchy. The chapter frames covenant as response to liberation and presence, with boundaries around overwhelming theophany.
